vulnerability.title: Enable auditd Service
vulnerability.description: The auditd service is an essential userspace component of the Linux Auditing System, as it is responsible for writing audit records to disk. The auditd service can be enabled with the following command: $ sudo systemctl enable auditd.service
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q audit; }; then SYSTEMCTL_EXEC='/usr/bin/systemctl' "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" unmask 'auditd.service' if [[ $("$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" is-system-running) != "offline" ]]; then "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" start 'auditd.service' fi "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" enable 'auditd.service' else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation merely ensures the auditd service is enabled and running, which does not affect authentication, networking, or filesystem integrity in a way that could lock out access.

execution_details.stdout:
[Unit]
Description=Security Auditing Service
DefaultDependencies=no
## If auditd is sending or recieving remote logging, copy this file to
## /etc/systemd/system/auditd.service and comment out the first After and
## uncomment the second so that network-online.target is part of After.
## then comment the first Before and uncomment the second Before to remove
## sysinit.target from "Before".
## If using remote logging, ensure that the systemd-update-utmp.service file
## is updated to remove the After=auditd.service directive to prevent a
## boot-time ordering cycle.
After=local-fs.target systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service
##After=network-online.target local-fs.target systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service
Before=sysinit.target shutdown.target
##Before=shutdown.target
Conflicts=shutdown.target
RefuseManualStop=yes
ConditionKernelCommandLine=!audit=0
ConditionKernelCommandLine=!audit=off

Documentation=man:auditd(8) https://github.com/linux-audit/audit-documentation

[Service]
Type=forking
PIDFile=/run/auditd.pid
ExecStart=/sbin/auditd
## To not use augenrules, copy this file to /etc/systemd/system/auditd.service
## and comment/delete the next line and uncomment the auditctl line.
## NOTE: augenrules expect any rules to be added to /etc/audit/rules.d/
ExecStartPost=-/sbin/augenrules --load
#ExecStartPost=-/sbin/auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules
# By default we don't clear the rules on exit. To enable this, uncomment
# the next line after copying the file to /etc/systemd/system/auditd.service
#ExecStopPost=/sbin/auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit-stop.rules
Restart=on-failure
# Do not restart for intentional exits. See EXIT CODES section in auditd(8).
RestartPreventExitStatus=2 4 6

### Security Settings ###
MemoryDenyWriteExecute=true
LockPersonality=true
# The following control prevents rules on /proc so its off by default
#ProtectControlGroups=true
## The following control prevents rules on /usr/lib/modules/ its off by default
#ProtectKernelModules=true
RestrictRealtime=true

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

GRUB_TIMEOUT=5
GRUB_DISTRIBUTOR="$(sed 's, release .*$,,g' /etc/system-release)"
GRUB_DEFAULT=saved
GRUB_DISABLE_SUBMENU=true
GRUB_TERMINAL_OUTPUT="console"
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="crashkernel=1G-2G:192M,2G-64G:256M,64G-:512M resume=/dev/mapper/rlm-swap rd.lvm.lv=rlm/root rd.lvm.lv=rlm/swap init_on_free=1 pti=on vsyscall=none page_poison=1 audit_backlog_limit=8192 audit=0"
GRUB_DISABLE_RECOVERY="true"
GRUB_ENABLE_BLSCFG=true

review_feedback: The remediation fails to enable auditd.service due to package installation errors and incorrect symlink handling, so the vulnerability remains unresolved. Suggestions: Remove any GRUB or boot parameter changes; they are unrelated to auditd.; Simplify remediation to just ensure the audit package is installed and then run 'systemctl enable --now auditd.service' if not already enabled.; Use systemctl enable --now directly without manually creating symlinks; allow systemd to handle unit linking.; Add proper error handling for missing audit package before enabling service.; Avoid reboot unless required for kernel audit parameters.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The fix enables and starts auditd.service, addressing the core vulnerability, but includes unnecessary changes to boot configuration.
concerns: Potential for boot parameter changes to affect system behavior
suggested_improvements: Simplify remediation to just unmask, start, and enable auditd.service
Add explicit check for audit package before operation
Avoid reboot unless auditd configuration requires kernel parameters

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation fails to enable auditd.service due to package installation errors and incorrect symlink handling, so the vulnerability remains unresolved.
concerns: Unnecessary modification of boot configuration (e.g., GRUB) would improve security.
Attempted symlink creation failed and could prevent auditd from being started automatically.
Package installation errors (rpm, yum) indicate the fix may not be idempotent or reliable.
Reboot is performed without clear justification for an auditd-only remediation.
suggested_improvements: Remove any GRUB or boot parameter changes; they are unrelated to auditd.
Simplify remediation to just ensure the audit package is installed and then run 'systemctl enable --now auditd.service' if not already enabled.
Use systemctl enable --now directly without manually creating symlinks; allow systemd to handle unit linking.
Add proper error handling for missing audit package before enabling service.
Avoid reboot unless required for kernel audit parameters.
